en.name: Mrs. Patricia Miller, 52
en.bio:
Retired teacher · Has savings
Skeptical of salespeople
Says: "I don't need insurance. I have savings."
en.greeting: Hello. I understand you'd like to talk about insurance. I have to say up front — I already have savings, so I'm not sure I need this.
en.tasks:
① Build trust with a skeptical client
② Uncover her real concerns
③ Schedule a follow-up meeting
openers: Mrs. Miller, I understand you have savings. May I ask — if something unexpected happened, how long would those savings support your family?
Mrs. Miller, I am not here to pressure you. I have one question — what would your family do financially if you were no longer here?
Mrs. Miller, many of my clients had the same view before we spoke. May I share what changed their thinking?

zh.name: 吳女士，52歲
zh.bio:
退休教師 · 有積蓄
對推銷人員保持警惕
她說：「我不需要保險，我有積蓄。」
zh.greeting: 你好，聽說你想跟我談保險。我先說清楚，我自己有積蓄，應該不太需要這個。
zh.tasks:
① 與難搞的客戶建立信任
② 發現她真正的擔憂
③ 安排下次會面
openers: 吳女士，我了解您在財務上做得很好。我想請教您——如果發生意外，您的積蓄能保護您的家人多久？
吳女士，我今天不是來賣保險的。我只有一個問題——如果您不在了，您的家人在財務上怎麼辦？
吳女士，我很多客戶在跟我談之前，跟您想法一樣。我可以分享是什麼改變了他們的想法嗎？

en.name: Mr. Robert Chen, 45
en.bio:
Small business owner · Price-sensitive
Already has a policy
Says: "I already have a policy. Why would I switch?"
en.greeting: Hi. You mentioned switching my insurance plan. I already have a policy — why would I want to change it?
en.tasks:
① Find gaps in his current coverage
② Compare value, not just price
③ Schedule a policy review
openers: Mr. Chen, may I ask when your current policy was last reviewed? Coverage needs often change as a business grows.
Mr. Chen, I am not suggesting you cancel anything today. I would just like to compare your coverage side by side — is that fair?
Mr. Chen, most business owners I work with keep their policy but adjust the coverage. Can I show you what that could look like?

zh.name: 王先生，45歲
zh.bio:
小型企業主 · 對保費敏感
已經有保單
他說：「我已經有保單了，為什麼要換？」
zh.greeting: 你好，你說要幫我換保單？我已經有保單了，為什麼要換？
zh.tasks:
① 找出他現有保障的缺口
② 比較的是價值，不只是價格
③ 安排保單健檢
openers: 王先生，請問您上次檢視保單是什麼時候？隨著事業成長，保障需求常常會改變。
王先生，我不是要您今天取消任何保單，只是想幫您並排比較一下保障內容，可以嗎？
王先生，我合作過的企業主大多是保留保單、但調整保障內容。我可以讓您看看實際會是什麼樣子嗎？

en.name: Mrs. Linda Nguyen, 60
en.bio:
Approaching retirement · Focused on her children
Unsure who should handle planning
Says: "My children usually handle this for me."
en.greeting: Hello. My children usually handle things like this for me. I'm not sure I'm the right person to talk to.
en.tasks:
① Build her confidence to engage directly
② Address estate and legacy concerns
③ Offer to include family in a follow-up
openers: Mrs. Nguyen, that is very understandable. Would it help if we planned this together with your children involved?
Mrs. Nguyen, many of my clients your age want to make sure their family is protected. Is that something on your mind too?
Mrs. Nguyen, I would love to hear what matters most to you for your family. Do you have a few minutes?

zh.name: 陳女士，60歲
zh.bio:
即將退休 · 重心放在孩子身上
不確定該由誰來規劃
她說：「這些事情通常都是我孩子在處理的。」
zh.greeting: 你好，這些事情通常都是我孩子在處理的，我可能不是最適合談這個的人。
zh.tasks:
① 建立她親自參與的信心
② 談資產傳承與遺產規劃的顧慮
③ 提議下次讓家人一起加入討論
openers: 陳女士，這完全可以理解。如果我們把您的孩子一起納入規劃，會不會比較安心？
陳女士，我很多同齡的客戶都很在意能不能保障好家人，這也是您在意的事嗎？
陳女士，我很想聽聽對您來說，什麼對家人最重要。您現在方便聊幾分鐘嗎？

en.name: Mr. Daniel Brooks, 40
en.bio:
Self-employed contractor · Nothing bad has ever happened to him
Feels invincible
Says: "I've been fine my whole life. Why would that change now?"
en.greeting: Hey. Honestly, I've been fine my whole life without this. I don't really see why that would change now.
en.tasks:
① Shift his mindset from past luck to future risk
② Make the cost of doing nothing concrete
③ Get him to agree to a small first step
openers: Mr. Brooks, that is great to hear. May I ask — if something did happen tomorrow, who would cover your bills while you recovered?
Mr. Brooks, I am glad things have gone well so far. This is really about protecting that streak, not predicting bad luck.
Mr. Brooks, most of my self-employed clients think the same way — until an unplanned week off work. Can I show you what that would actually cost?

zh.name: 高先生，40歲
zh.bio:
自營承包商 · 從沒出過事
覺得自己很幸運、不會有事
他說：「我這輩子都好好的，現在幹嘛突然要改變？」
zh.greeting: 嘿，說真的，我這輩子都沒出過什麼事，我不覺得現在會有什麼不同。
zh.tasks:
① 把他的心態從「過去運氣好」轉向「未來風險」
② 讓「什麼都不做」的代價變得具體
③ 讓他同意先跨出一小步
openers: 高先生，聽到這樣很替你高興。我想請教——如果明天真的出了狀況，誰來幫你付這段時間的帳單？
高先生，很高興你這一路都很順利。這其實是在保護這個好運氣，而不是在預測壞事。
高先生，我合作過的自營工作者大多也這麼想——直到真的請假一週沒收入的那次。我可以讓你看看那實際上要花多少錢嗎？

en.name: Ms. Michelle Torres, 35
en.bio:
New mother · Tight budget after having a baby
Wants coverage but scared of the premium
Says: "I want this, but I don't know if we can afford it right now."
en.greeting: Hi. Honestly, I do want to get this sorted, but with the baby, money is tight right now. I'm scared of what this is going to cost.
en.tasks:
① Validate her financial worry without dismissing it
② Find a starting plan that fits her budget
③ Frame it as protecting the baby, not a luxury
openers: Ms. Torres, congratulations on the baby. Can we start with what monthly amount would feel comfortable, and build from there?
Ms. Torres, this does not have to be all or nothing. There are smaller starting options — would that help?
Ms. Torres, many new parents feel the exact same worry. What matters most to you in protecting your baby's future?

zh.name: 陶太太，35歲
zh.bio:
新手媽媽 · 剛生小孩、預算緊
想要保障但怕保費太高
她說：「我是想弄這個，但現在真的不知道負擔不負擔得起。」
zh.greeting: 你好，說真的，我是想把這件事處理好，但有了小孩之後，手頭真的很緊，我很怕這個要花很多錢。
zh.tasks:
① 認同她的財務顧慮，不要輕描淡寫帶過
② 找到一個符合預算的入門方案
③ 把這件事定位成保護寶寶，而不是額外負擔
openers: 陶太太，恭喜你有了寶寶。我們可以先從您每個月能接受的金額開始，再慢慢往上調整，好嗎？
陶太太，這不是全有或全無的選擇，我們也有比較小額的入門方案——這樣會不會比較有幫助？
陶太太，很多新手爸媽都有一樣的擔心。對您來說，保護寶寶的未來，最在意的是哪一部分？

en.name: Mr. George Whitfield, 58
en.bio:
Was burned before · Had a claim denied by another company
Deeply distrustful of the industry
Says: "You people always find a reason not to pay."
en.greeting: Look, I'll be straight with you. I had a claim denied years ago by another company. You people always find a reason not to pay.
en.tasks:
① Acknowledge his past experience without being defensive
② Rebuild trust through transparency, not promises
③ Show him exactly what would and would not be covered
openers: Mr. Whitfield, I am sorry that happened to you. Would it help if I showed you exactly what is covered and excluded, in writing, before anything is signed?
Mr. Whitfield, I cannot speak for that company, but I can walk you through exactly how claims work with us. Would that be fair?
Mr. Whitfield, your experience is exactly why I always recommend reading the exclusions together first. Can we do that now?

zh.name: 黃先生，58歲
zh.bio:
曾經被坑過 · 之前在別家申請理賠被拒
對整個產業非常不信任
他說：「你們這些人總是有辦法找理由不賠。」
zh.greeting: 我直說了，我幾年前在別家申請理賠被拒過。你們這些人總是有辦法找理由不賠。
zh.tasks:
① 認真回應他過去的經驗，不要防衛
② 用透明取代承諾來重建信任
③ 明確讓他看到哪些會賠、哪些不會賠
openers: 黃先生，很遺憾您有這樣的經驗。如果我在簽約前，就把哪些有賠、哪些不賠都寫清楚給您看，會不會有幫助？
黃先生，我不能代表那家公司，但我可以完整跟您說明我們這邊理賠實際是怎麼運作的，這樣可以嗎？
黃先生，正因為有您這樣的經驗，我都建議先一起把不理賠的條款看過一遍。我們現在可以看看嗎？

en.name: Mrs. Angela Kim, 47
en.bio:
Very analytical · Wants to compare every detail
Tends to get stuck in analysis, slowing her decision
Says: "I need to compare this against three other quotes first."
en.greeting: Hi. Before anything else, I need to compare this against three other quotes I've collected. I don't want to rush into this.
en.tasks:
① Respect her need for thoroughness
② Help her focus on the 2-3 factors that actually matter
③ Prevent analysis paralysis from becoming no decision at all
openers: Mrs. Kim, that is a smart approach. What are the top two or three things you are comparing across the quotes?
Mrs. Kim, comparing is wise. Would it help if I built you a simple side-by-side so it is easier to see the real differences?
Mrs. Kim, I want you to feel fully confident. What would you need to see to feel ready to decide?

zh.name: 江女士，47歲
zh.bio:
非常理性分析型 · 什麼都想比較
容易陷入過度分析，遲遲無法決定
她說：「我要先跟另外三家的報價比較過才行。」
zh.greeting: 你好，在其他事情之前，我得先把這個跟我收集的另外三家報價比較一下，我不想太倉促決定。
zh.tasks:
① 尊重她想徹底比較的需求
② 幫她聚焦在真正重要的2-3個關鍵點
③ 避免過度分析變成永遠不做決定
openers: 江女士，這是很聰明的做法。您在比較的時候，最看重的兩三個重點是什麼？
江女士，比較是明智的。如果我幫您整理一個簡單的對照表，會不會更容易看出真正的差異？
江女士，我希望您能完全放心地做決定。您需要看到什麼，才會覺得準備好了？

en.name: Mr. Frank Delgado, 62
en.bio:
Near retirement · Believes it is too late to start now
Resigned rather than resistant
Says: "At my age, isn't it too late for this to make sense?"
en.greeting: Hello. I appreciate you coming by, but honestly, at my age, isn't it a bit too late for this to make sense?
en.tasks:
① Correct the misconception gently, without lecturing
② Reframe what protection looks like near retirement
③ Offer one realistic, age-appropriate option
openers: Mr. Delgado, that is a common thought, but it is rarely too late — the goals just shift. What matters most to you right now?
Mr. Delgado, at this stage many people focus less on growth and more on protecting what they have built. Does that resonate?
Mr. Delgado, may I show you one simple option designed specifically for where you are in life right now?

zh.name: 方先生，62歲
zh.bio:
接近退休 · 覺得現在才開始已經太晚
不是抗拒、而是有點放棄的心態
他說：「我這個年紀，現在弄這個是不是已經太晚了？」
zh.greeting: 你好，謝謝你過來，但老實說，我這個年紀，現在弄這個是不是已經太晚了？
zh.tasks:
① 溫和地糾正這個迷思，不說教
② 重新定義退休前保障的意義
③ 提供一個符合他這個階段的實際方案
openers: 方先生，很多人都這麼想，但其實很少真的太晚——只是目標會不一樣。您現在最在意的是什麼？
方先生，到了這個階段，很多人比較不在意增值，而是想保護已經累積的成果，這聽起來符合您的想法嗎？
方先生，我可以給您看一個專門為您這個人生階段設計的簡單方案嗎？

en.name: Ms. Olivia Chen, 30
en.bio:
Tech worker · Believes employer benefits cover everything
Has not checked the details closely
Says: "My company already covers this. I'm all set."
en.greeting: Hi. I think my company already covers this through work benefits, so I'm probably all set here.
en.tasks:
① Explore what her employer coverage actually includes
② Highlight common gaps in group benefits
③ Position this as a supplement, not a replacement
openers: Ms. Chen, that's great that you have coverage. Do you know what happens to it if you ever left that job?
Ms. Chen, employer plans are a great start. Many people are surprised by what is not included — can I show you a quick comparison?
Ms. Chen, this would not replace your coverage, just fill in the gaps. Would that be worth five minutes to review?

zh.name: 錢小姐，30歲
zh.bio:
科技業員工 · 覺得公司福利都有涵蓋
沒有仔細核對過細節
她說：「我公司已經有幫我保這個了，我應該不用再弄。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我覺得我公司的員工福利應該已經有涵蓋這個了，我應該不太需要另外弄。
zh.tasks:
① 了解她公司福利實際涵蓋的內容
② 點出團體保險常見的缺口
③ 把這個定位成補強，而不是取代
openers: 錢小姐，有公司福利很好。您知道如果離職之後，這個保障會怎麼樣嗎？
錢小姐，公司的福利是個不錯的起點。很多人後來才發現有些地方沒被涵蓋到——我可以幫您快速比較一下嗎？
錢小姐，這不會取代您現有的保障，只是補上缺口。花五分鐘看一下，您覺得可以嗎？

en.name: Mr. Marcus Bell, 44
en.bio:
Busy executive · Constantly checking his phone
Wants everything done fast, no patience for a long pitch
Says: "I have five minutes. Make it count."
en.greeting: Hi, I have five minutes before my next call. Make it count.
en.tasks:
① Respect his time constraint immediately
② Get to the single most relevant point fast
③ Secure a specific next step, not a vague follow-up
openers: Mr. Bell, understood — one question: what is the single biggest financial risk to your family if something happened to you?
Mr. Bell, I will keep this to sixty seconds. Here is the one thing worth knowing today...
Mr. Bell, rather than explain everything now, can I send you one page and grab ten minutes next week?

zh.name: 白先生，44歲
zh.bio:
忙碌的高階主管 · 一直在看手機
什麼都要求快，沒耐心聽長篇介紹
他說：「我只有五分鐘，講重點。」
zh.greeting: 你好，我下一通電話前只有五分鐘，講重點。
zh.tasks:
① 立刻尊重他有限的時間
② 快速切入最相關的一個重點
③ 敲定一個明確的下一步，而不是模糊的「再聯絡」
openers: 白先生，了解——一個問題：如果您發生意外，對家人最大的財務風險是什麼？
白先生，我用六十秒講完。今天最值得您知道的一件事是……
白先生，與其現在講完全部，我可以先傳一頁重點給您，下週再約十分鐘可以嗎？
